title: Safe Divide
description: Define an algebraic data type Result with two constructors: Failure (no payload) and Success(Int). Write a function that performs integer division of the first parameter by the second. If the divisor is zero, construct Failure; otherwise construct Success with the quotient. Use pattern matching on the Result to return -1 for Failure or the quotient for Success.
description_neutral: Perform integer division, returning the quotient if the divisor is non-zero, or -1 if division by zero. Internally, represent the outcome as an algebraic data type with success and failure variants, and use pattern matching to extract the result.
notes: Parameters: safe_divide(a, b). @Int.0=b (divisor), @Int.1=a (dividend). The function must define a private Result ADT, construct it based on the divisor, and pattern match to extract the value. Inside Success(@Int), @Int.0 refers to the matched quotient.
